data.body:
FINAL EVIDENCE (both gates).
CODE-REVIEW live re-runs (6 real merged diffs, current gate/prompts): 31 LLM findings, all validity=1.0, IMPACT low-moderate => priority min0.19/p50 0.41/mean0.44/MAX 0.67. would-block @0.70 = ZERO; @0.60 = 2/31. So LLM code-review blocking at any responsible bar (>=0.70) is a NO-OP; lowering to ~0.58 would block moderate-impact maintainability/tests/docs coaching (the class adjudication says keep advisory). Verdict=BLOCK on all 6 was a LOCAL artifact (DET secret/high-crit detectors timed out -> fail-closed-abstain; they have time on server). Eval (runs=1 smoke): code-review-security recall0.857 FA0.000 kappa0.833 (safe-looking) but security findings didn't even fire on the 6 diffs, and DET high-critical-security already covers that class. CONCLUSION code-review: KEEP all 13 LLM advisory; only DET secret-detection+high-critical-security block (status quo). Introducing LLM blocking unwarranted.
PLAN-REVIEW adjudication rollup (47 would-block findings across 2 rounds): block-worthy% = G6 88%, COH 20%, T5e 20%, E2 ~12%, G5 0%(+FP), T4 0%, F1 0%. Only G6(approach-soundness, code-grounded) reliably block-worthy; rest = accurate process/completeness coaching (specify-mechanism/decompose-story/add-rollback/add-ADR) better as advisory. G5 has epic-vs-story altitude bug + an outright FP. PROPOSAL plan-review: KEEP G6 blocking@0.70 (flip-optimal, 88% precise); DEMOTE E2,G5,T4,COH,F1,T5e -> advisory@0.95. Net block-rate ~24%->~19% (G6 drives 127/148 blocks anyway); removes the low-precision process over-blocks. Numeric 0.70 stays (0 FP, minimizes verdict-flip). Root cause of over-block: 'accurate+critical-severity' != 'block-worthy'; blocking should track code-groundedness.
